summary.brief: BOURNE v. VALDES, M.D.. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: Respondent Dr. Zidrieck Valdes diagnosed his patient, David Bourne, with chronic anxiety, major depressive disorder, and chronic low back pain in 2015.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Valdes prescribed Bourne two medications: Klonopin (a benzodiazepine) and an opioid.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In May 2019, Valdes informed Bourne that a new Center for Disease Control guideline recommended that a patient not be prescribed benzodiazepines and opioids concurrently.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Valdes took Bourne off Klonopin and prescribed Bourne Buspar, an anti-anxiety medication.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Bourne's wife overheard Bourne inform Valdes that he was having withdrawal symptoms as a result of being taken off Klonopin.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Valdes then referred Bourne to an in-patient drug treatment facility, but Bourne refused to go.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In August 2019, Bourne reported to Valdes that he was experiencing 'unbearable' work-related stress.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Valdes gave Bourne a half-dose emergency prescription of Klonopin.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Medical notes reflect that Bourne 'displayed no physical or mental symptoms of withdrawal arising from the Klonopin.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Bourne 'exhibited no signs or symptoms of being depressed or suicidal.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In November 2019, shortly after one of his medical visits with Valdes, Bourne died by suicide.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In his suicide note, Bourne wrote, 'my mind is gone all from being prescribed a drug I didn't even need ... the Dr. stopped prescribing without tapering . . . .'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: At the time of his death, Bourne had therapeutic levels of both Xanax and an opioid in his system.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Valdes had not prescribed Bourne Xanax and was unaware that Bourne was taking it.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Appellants' medical expert, Dr. Donald A. Misch, opined inter alia that Valdes deviated from the standard of care when he simultaneously prescribed an opioid and a benzodiazepine, and when he took Bourne off Klonopin without tapering the dosage.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Donald A. Misch

evidence_index.text: Dr. Misch further opined that Valdes' actions contributed to Bourne's suicide.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Donald A. Misch

evidence_index.text: We decline to adopt a rule that a patient's suicide relieves a medical provider of liability for the patient's death.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We instead hold that the determination as to whether a medical provider is liable for a patient's injuries must be resolved under established medical malpractice law.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The district court erroneously found as a matter of law that Valdes could not be liable for Bourne's death by suicide because Valdes did not have control or custody over Bourne and therefore could not have stopped the suicide.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Because appellants established a prima facie medical malpractice claim, summary judgment was improper without further analysis of whether there was a genuine issue of material fact regarding the suicide's foreseeability.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.chief_complaint: Chronic anxiety, major depressive disorder, and chronic low back pain
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient diagnosed with chronic anxiety, major depressive disorder, and chronic low back pain in 2015. Prescribed Klonopin and an opioid. In May 2019, informed about new CDC guidelines and taken off Klonopin, prescribed Buspar instead.

focused_exam.mental_status: No physical or mental symptoms of withdrawal or suicidal ideation noted

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Management of chronic anxiety and depressive disorder

requestables.name: Psychiatric Evaluation
requestables.description: Evaluation of mental health status and medication management

reveal.result_summary: Patient reported no suicidal ideation, Buspar effective for anxiety
reveal.decision_impact: Supports continuation of current medication regimen

timeline_phases.name: Initial Diagnosis and Treatment
timeline_phases.description: Patient diagnosed and treated with Klonopin and opioid

key_events.event: Diagnosis of chronic anxiety and depressive disorder

timeline_phases.name: Medication Adjustment
timeline_phases.description: Adjustment of medication following CDC guidelines

timeline_phases.name: Subsequent Management
timeline_phases.description: Management of reported stress and withdrawal symptoms

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with chronic anxiety and depressive disorder on Klonopin and opioid
decision_points.prompt: How should the medication regimen be adjusted in light of new CDC guidelines?

options.description: Taper off Klonopin gradually while monitoring for withdrawal symptoms
Immediately stop Klonopin and switch to Buspar
options.clinical_reasoning: Abrupt cessation can lead to withdrawal symptoms

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant stopped Klonopin and prescribed Buspar

expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence indicated tapering is standard

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Abrupt cessation led to withdrawal symptoms
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Tapering could have prevented withdrawal symptoms

factual_timeline.event: Patient diagnosed with chronic anxiety and depressive disorder

diagnoses.diagnosis: Chronic anxiety and major depressive disorder
